sections.paragraph:
ϑλίβω [orig. unclear; basic idea is one of compression ‘squeeze, chafe’]

—1. press close, crowd Mk 3:9 (of a dense group of pers.).

—2. make narrow Mt 7:14 (a road).

—3. metaph., squeeze, oppress, afflict 2 Th 1:6; mostly pass. 2 Cor 1:6; 4:8; 7:5; 1 Th 3:4; 2 Th 1:7; 1 Ti 5:10; Hb 11:37. Wordplay: ἐν παντὶ ϑλιβόμενοι ἀλλ’ οὐ στενοχωρούμενοι constantly squeezed but not cooped up 2 Cor 4:8.
